rationale: Recent exchange shutdown / crisis watch batch covering Bit.com, Drift Protocol, Gemini regional exit, ProBit Global, and Fastex Exchange. Bit.com and ProBit Global are modeled as dead-side orderly wind-downs. Drift Protocol is modeled as limited after a major exploit. Gemini remains active and receives regional exit events only. Fastex Exchange is modeled as dead / regulation because official Fastex-domain pages display a notice stating that exchange operations halt on April 1 at 23:59 Yerevan time; the regulatory / MiCA rationale is supported by secondary reporting, so confidence remains medium.

entities.summary: Matrixport-linked centralized exchange and derivatives venue launched in 2020 that entered a phased shutdown in December 2025, with trading functions ending in early 2026 and a final user-asset migration / withdrawal deadline on March 31, 2026.
entities.notes: Death reason is mapped to voluntary_shutdown because available evidence describes an orderly three-phase wind-down and user asset migration plan rather than insolvency, hack, or enforcement. Source coverage is mostly secondary / mirrored exchange news; upgrade with archived official Bit.com notice if found.

entities.summary: Solana-based decentralized perpetuals and trading protocol that entered a critical limited state in April 2026 after a major exploit and suspension of deposits and withdrawals.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead yet. The April 2026 exploit and deposit / withdrawal suspension are material enough for HEI because they changed platform availability, but no final shutdown marker is confirmed.

entities.summary: Centralized exchange that announced a full wind-down of exchange services, ending spot trading in January 2026 and terminating standard services on February 26, 2026, with a delayed asset-return period into April 2026.
entities.notes: Death reason is mapped to voluntary_shutdown because available materials describe an orderly service termination driven by regulatory environment and strategic restructuring, not a direct enforcement shutdown. The later 2026-04-01 asset-return / forfeiture deadline is tracked as a separate post-shutdown event.

entities.summary: Fastex ecosystem centralized exchange that entered a withdrawal-only / service-termination process and whose official-domain pages stated that exchange operations would halt on April 1, 2026 at 23:59 Yerevan time.
entities.notes: Classified as dead / regulation rather than limited because official Fastex-domain pages displayed a termination notice for exchange operations. MiCA / regulatory alignment is reported as the driver by secondary coverage, while official-domain pages are used for the terminal operations marker. Confidence remains medium because the reason is supported indirectly.

events.title: Bit.com announced a phased shutdown and user asset migration plan
events.description: Bit.com began a three-phase wind-down on December 27, 2025, disabled new user registrations, and directed users to withdraw assets or migrate balances to Matrixport.
events.notes: The available evidence describes an orderly wind-down rather than an insolvency or hack event.

events.title: Bit.com spot trading scheduled to end
events.description: The wind-down plan scheduled the end of spot trading for January 31, 2026, followed by restrictions on contract trading and withdrawal-only handling.
events.notes: Trading halt marker follows the shutdown timeline reported by multiple secondary sources.

events.title: Bit.com final shutdown deadline reached
events.description: The phased shutdown plan concluded with a final migration / withdrawal deadline of March 31, 2026.
events.notes: HEI uses the final migration / withdrawal deadline as the terminal marker.

events.title: Drift Protocol suffered a major exploit
events.description: Drift Protocol confirmed an active attack on April 1, 2026; public estimates ranged from over $130 million to about $285 million, with later analysis describing privileged-access and fake-token collateral mechanisms.
events.notes: Use exploit rather than hack because this is a DeFi / DEX protocol incident involving protocol control and collateral manipulation.

events.title: Drift suspended deposits and withdrawals
events.description: After confirming the active attack, Drift suspended deposits and withdrawals while coordinating containment with security firms, bridges, and exchanges.
events.notes: This is the state-changing event that justifies the limited classification pending final recovery or shutdown evidence.

events.title: Gemini announced UK, EEA, and Australia account closures
events.description: Gemini announced it would close customer accounts in the United Kingdom, European Economic Area, and Australia, with a phased regional wind-down ending on April 6, 2026.
events.notes: Gemini remains active globally; this is a regional-exit event, not a dead-side entity reclassification.

events.title: Gemini regional accounts entered withdrawal-only mode
events.description: Affected UK, EEA, and Australia customer accounts entered withdrawal-only handling before final regional closure.
events.notes: Australian fiat withdrawal handling had an earlier February 28 / March 1 transition, but HEI uses the broader March 5 marker for regional withdrawal-only handling.

events.title: Gemini closed customer accounts in selected regions
events.description: Gemini closed customer accounts in the UK, EEA, and Australia on April 6, 2026, while the global Gemini exchange entity remained active.
events.notes: Regional service termination only; Gemini entity status stays active.

events.title: ProBit Global announced full service termination
events.description: ProBit Global announced a complete service wind-down, citing a rapidly evolving regulatory environment and strategic business restructuring.
events.notes: The announcement date is taken from multiple mirrored / secondary references to the official notice.

events.title: ProBit Global spot trading ended
events.description: ProBit Global's shutdown roadmap ended spot trading and delisted all spot pairs on January 28, 2026.
events.notes: Time-zone conversions differ across syndicated reports, but the date-level marker is consistent.

events.title: ProBit Global standard services terminated
events.description: ProBit Global's standard platform services and normal withdrawal window ended on February 26, 2026, with later asset-return handling continuing into April.
events.notes: HEI uses 2026-02-26 as the terminal exchange-service marker while preserving the later asset-return deadline as a separate event.

events.title: ProBit Global delayed asset-return period ended
events.description: Reports of the ProBit Global wind-down describe a delayed asset-return period ending on April 1, 2026, after which unclaimed assets could be considered abandoned under the exchange terms.
events.notes: This is retained as post-shutdown custody / asset-return context rather than the core exchange-service terminal date.

events.title: Fastex Exchange service termination notice reported
events.description: A February 2026 report described a Fastex Exchange service-termination notice and withdrawal-only state attributed to MiCA / regulatory alignment.
events.notes: Month-level proxy date for the reported service-termination notice. Secondary reporting provides the regulatory / MiCA context; official-domain pages are attached to the effective halt event.

events.title: Fastex Exchange termination deadline reported
events.description: The reported service-termination notice set April 1, 2026 at 00:00 Yerevan Time as the terminal deadline for exchange operations.
events.notes: Official Fastex-domain pages state that exchange operations will halt on April 1 at 23:59 Yerevan time.

evidence.title: Bit.com Announces Three-Phase Shutdown Plan to March 31, 2026
evidence.notes: Used for the Dec 27 shutdown announcement, new-registration halt, Matrixport migration, Jan 31 spot-trading end, and Mar 31 final deadline.

evidence.title: Bit.com to Wind Down Operations by March 31, 2026 — Users Urged to Withdraw or Convert to USDT
evidence.notes: Used for the three-phase timeline, Jan 31 trading cutoff, final withdrawal window, and Matrixport migration context.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange Bit.com set to wind down operations
evidence.notes: Supporting source for trading shutdown and migration timeline.

evidence.title: De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
evidence.notes: Used for Drift's active-attack confirmation and deposit / withdrawal suspension.

evidence.title: The Drift Protocol Hack: How Privileged Access Led to a $285 Million Loss
evidence.notes: Used for exploit mechanics, estimated loss amount, and privileged-access context.

evidence.title: Drift Protocol Hit by $285M Exploit
evidence.notes: Supporting source for exploit amount estimates and protocol classification as a Solana decentralized perpetuals exchange.

evidence.title: Gemini closing accounts in the UK, EU, and Australia - Everything you need to know
evidence.notes: Primary source for the regional account closures, withdrawal-only dates, and April 6 closure deadline.

evidence.title: Gemini Payments UK Ltd and Gemini Intergalactic UK Ltd exit the UK market
evidence.notes: Regulatory confirmation of the UK exit, withdrawal-only mode from March 5, and April 6 account closure.

evidence.title: Gemini crypto exchange axes Australian accounts, cuts 200 jobs
evidence.notes: Supporting source for the Australia account closure and broader market exit context.

evidence.title: [IMPORTANT] ProBit Global Service Termination
evidence.notes: Mirror of the ProBit Global service-termination notice; used for roadmap dates, termination cause, and withdrawal deadlines.

evidence.title: ProBit Global Announces Full Service Termination by February 26, 2026
evidence.notes: Supporting source for spot trading end, service termination, asset-return period, and abandoned-asset deadline.

evidence.title: ProBit Global Announces Termination of All Services by April 2026
evidence.notes: Supporting source for the April 1 final asset-return / website-closure context.

evidence.title: Fastex Exchange Announces Full Service Termination by April 2026
evidence.notes: Thin source for the reported withdrawal-only / April 1 service-termination notice. Keep record limited until stronger official evidence is found.

evidence.title: Fastex trade volume and market listings
evidence.notes: Used for Fastex identity, official exchange URL, launch date, and origin context. The continued presence of market data is one reason HEI keeps the record limited rather than dead.

evidence.title: Account Verification
evidence.notes: Official Fastex-domain page displayed a service termination notice stating that exchange operations will halt on April 1 at 23:59 Yerevan time.

evidence.title: Listing Rules
evidence.notes: Official Fastex-domain page displayed the same exchange-operation halt notice and post-deadline account fee warning.

manual_review_checklist: Bit.com is accepted as dead / voluntary_shutdown / medium because multiple secondary sources agree on a phased shutdown timeline.
Drift Protocol is accepted as limited / exploit / medium and must not be marked dead without a final shutdown marker.
Gemini receives regional exit events only; do not change the Gemini entity status from active.
ProBit Global is accepted as dead / voluntary_shutdown / medium because shutdown dates and asset-return process are consistent across sources.
Fastex Exchange is accepted as dead / regulation / medium because official-domain pages confirm the halt of exchange operations, while the regulatory cause is supported by secondary coverage.
Remove one-off apply workflow and trigger file after canonical application.
